candidate_problem_record.subject: math
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: As illustrated, let $\triangle ABC$ be an equilateral triangle. Draw a perpendicular from point $D$ on side $AB$ to $BC$ at point $E$. Construct $EF \perp DE$, intersecting $AC$ at point $F$. Draw $FG \perp AC$, meeting $BC$ at point $G$, where $FG$ and $DE$ intersect at point $M$. If $DM = ME$ and $GE = 3$, what is the length of $AB$?
candidate_problem_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} As shown in the figure, draw MN $\perp$ BC at point N, \\
$\because \triangle ABC$ is an equilateral triangle, \\
$\therefore \angle B=\angle C=60^\circ$, \\
$\because DE\perp AB$, $EF\perp DE$, $FG\perp AC$, \\
$\therefore \angle BDE=90^\circ$, $\angle DEF=90^\circ$, $\angle GFC=90^\circ$, \\
$\therefore \angle DEB=180^\circ-90^\circ-60^\circ=30^\circ$, $\angle FGC=180^\circ-90^\circ-60^\circ=30^\circ$, \\
$\therefore \angle FEC=180^\circ-90^\circ-30^\circ=60^\circ$, $\angle DEB=\angle FGC$, \\
$\therefore GN=EN=\frac{1}{2}GE=\frac{3}{2}$, \\
In $Rt\triangle MNE$, $\angle MEN=30^\circ$, $EN=\frac{3}{2}$, \\
$\therefore \frac{NE}{ME}=\cos30^\circ=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$, \\
$\therefore ME=\frac{\frac{3}{2}}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}=\sqrt{3}$, \\
$\because DM=ME$, \\
$\therefore DE=2ME=2\sqrt{3}$, \\
In $Rt\triangle BDE$, $\angle DEB=30^\circ$, \\
$\therefore BE=\frac{DE}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}=\frac{2\sqrt{3}}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}=4$, \\
$\because \angle C=60^\circ$, $\angle FEC=60^\circ$, \\
$\therefore \triangle EFC$ is an equilateral triangle, \\
$\therefore \angle EFC=60^\circ$, $EF=CE$, \\
$\therefore \angle GFE=90^\circ-60^\circ=30^\circ=\angle FGC$, \\
$\therefore EF=GE=3$, \\
$\therefore CE=3$, \\
$\therefore BC=BE+CE=4+3=7$, \\
$\therefore AB=BC=\boxed{7}$, \\
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集被视为具备较稳定的多解潜力，可进入更强的多解挖掘链路。

clean_problem_record.normalized_question_text: As illustrated, let $\triangle ABC$ be an equilateral triangle. Draw a perpendicular from point $D$ on side $AB$ to $BC$ at point $E$. Construct $EF \perp DE$, intersecting $AC$ at point $F$. Draw $FG \perp AC$, meeting $BC$ at point $G$, where $FG$ and $DE$ intersect at point $M$. If $DM = ME$ and $GE = 3$, what is the length of $AB$?
clean_problem_record.normalized_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} As shown in the figure, draw MN $\perp$ BC at point N, \\
$\because \triangle ABC$ is an equilateral triangle, \\
$\therefore \angle B=\angle C=60^\circ$, \\
$\because DE\perp AB$, $EF\perp DE$, $FG\perp AC$, \\
$\therefore \angle BDE=90^\circ$, $\angle DEF=90^\circ$, $\angle GFC=90^\circ$, \\
$\therefore \angle DEB=180^\circ-90^\circ-60^\circ=30^\circ$, $\angle FGC=180^\circ-90^\circ-60^\circ=30^\circ$, \\
$\therefore \angle FEC=180^\circ-90^\circ-30^\circ=60^\circ$, $\angle DEB=\angle FGC$, \\
$\therefore GN=EN=\frac{1}{2}GE=\frac{3}{2}$, \\
In $Rt\triangle MNE$, $\angle MEN=30^\circ$, $EN=\frac{3}{2}$, \\
$\therefore \frac{NE}{ME}=\cos30^\circ=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$, \\
$\therefore ME=\frac{\frac{3}{2}}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}=\sqrt{3}$, \\
$\because DM=ME$, \\
$\therefore DE=2ME=2\sqrt{3}$, \\
In $Rt\triangle BDE$, $\angle DEB=30^\circ$, \\
$\therefore BE=\frac{DE}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}=\frac{2\sqrt{3}}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}=4$, \\
$\because \angle C=60^\circ$, $\angle FEC=60^\circ$, \\
$\therefore \triangle EFC$ is an equilateral triangle, \\
$\therefore \angle EFC=60^\circ$, $EF=CE$, \\
$\therefore \angle GFE=90^\circ-60^\circ=30^\circ=\angle FGC$, \\
$\therefore EF=GE=3$, \\
$\therefore CE=3$, \\
$\therefore BC=BE+CE=4+3=7$, \\
$\therefore AB=BC=\boxed{7}$, \\

$\therefore \frac{NE}{ME}=\cos30^\circ=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$, \\
$\therefore ME=\frac{\frac{3}{2}}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}=\sqrt{3}$, \\
$\because DM=ME$, \\
$\therefore DE=2ME=2\sqrt{3}$, \\
In $Rt\triangle BDE$, $\angle DEB=30^\circ$, \\
$\therefore BE=\frac{DE}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}=\frac{2\sqrt{3}}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}=4$, \\
$\because \angle C=60^\circ$, $\angle FEC=60^\circ$, \\
$\therefore \triangle EFC$ is an equilateral triangle, \\
$\therefore \angle EFC=60^\circ$, $EF=CE$, \\
$\therefore \angle GFE=90^\circ-60^\circ=30^\circ=\angle FGC$, \\
$\therefore EF=GE=3$, \\
$\therefore CE=3$, \\
$\therefore BC=BE+CE=4+3=7$, \\
$\therefore AB=BC=\boxed{7}$, \\
